storyTitle: The Full Arc: Elenya's Ritual of Release and Return
description: Before the collapse, Elenya loved Malrik. After, she chose to sever the memory to survive. This arc reveals that choice, its cost, and the paradox of rebuilding connection with someone she deliberately forgot.

acts.actTitle: The Older Student and the Professor
acts.description: Elenya encounters Malrik's precision and becomes fascinated by his struggle to control meaning through structure.

segments.title: Between Memory and Verification
segments.dialogueContext: After Malrik's lecture on cultural memory preservation
segments.npcLine: You teach that structures preserve memory. But do you believe memory is only what can be recorded?

requiredGates.description: Player has met Elenya at least once

playerChoices.playerLine: He seems very committed to what he believes. Do you think that's wisdom or fear?
playerChoices.elenyaResponse: Perhaps the truest fear is the fear of what can't be measured. He's trying so hard to find solid ground.

playerChoices.playerLine: You're trying to teach him something he's not ready to learn.
playerChoices.elenyaResponse: Not ready. Or perhaps... afraid to learn. But he listened. That matters.

segments.title: The Seed of Challenge
segments.dialogueContext: After class, Elenya speaks to Malrik directly
segments.npcLine: Some memories live in the body, not the mind. It is if you've ever loved someone.

requiredGates.description: Completed first classroom observation
Requires narrative presence to witness this subtle challenge

playerChoices.playerLine: You're speaking to him as if you know him already.
playerChoices.elenyaResponse: I don't. But I recognize the armor. Love always lives beneath it.

playerChoices.playerLine: You're leaving him something to reach for.
playerChoices.elenyaResponse: Everyone deserves to know that presence matters. That being known matters. Even people who think they don't.

acts.description: Elenya and Malrik discover they both seek the lighthouse. Their sanctuary becomes shared.

segments.title: An Expected Presence
segments.dialogueContext: Elenya meditates at the lighthouse, Malrik arrives
segments.npcLine: I came here to sit with the stillness. I didn't expect to find anyone—but somehow I'm not surprised.

requiredGates.description: Completed Act 1 foundation
Minimum empathy to understand her spiritual presence

playerChoices.playerLine: You knew he'd come. You were waiting.
playerChoices.elenyaResponse: I wasn't waiting. But I knew. Just as I know you understand why.

playerChoices.playerLine: This place is sacred to both of you. That's why it matters.
playerChoices.elenyaResponse: Sacred space doesn't need agreement. It just needs two people willing to be present in it.

segments.title: Presence Without Explanation
segments.dialogueContext: Weeks of silent lunches together at the lighthouse
segments.npcLine: He doesn't ask me to explain myself. I don't ask him to change. We've simply learned that silence between two people can be full.

requiredGates.description: Elenya has held sacred space with Malrik
High empathy to understand the intimacy of shared silence

playerChoices.playerLine: You've changed him. He's lighter here.
playerChoices.elenyaResponse: Not changed. Remembered. He remembers how to be before the world told him to defend.

acts.description: Elenya realizes she loves him, but loves him in her way—without possession, with gentle holding.

segments.title: The Softening
segments.dialogueContext: Elenya observes Malrik's changes and feels her own transformation
segments.npcLine: I didn't know I could love someone whose worldview contradicted mine so completely. And yet—his certainty was beautiful because it was his. Not because it was right.

requiredGates.description: Elenya has recognized love beginning
Player must have balanced emotional perspective

playerChoices.playerLine: Love that doesn't need to change the other person—that's its own kind of wisdom.
playerChoices.elenyaResponse: Yes. I learned that with him. That presence is enough. That witnessing is a form of devotion.

segments.title: The Covenant of Presence
segments.dialogueContext: Elenya and Malrik's love deepens silently, without words
segments.npcLine: We never spoke the word. We never needed to. Our love lived in how we were together. In the way he let me teach him that presence matters. In the way I let him show me that structure can be a form of care.

requiredGates.description: Elenya has named her love
High observation to notice the grace of their unspoken covenant

playerChoices.playerLine: What you created together—that was real. Whether words were spoken or not.
playerChoices.elenyaResponse: It was. And because it was real, when it was taken, it left a mark that couldn't be erased. Even when I tried.

acts.description: The world breaks. Elenya wakes with an unbearable ache and a half-memory she can't reconcile.

segments.title: The Ache Without a Name
segments.dialogueContext: Post-collapse. Elenya awakens with no conscious memory but with unbearable emotional pain
segments.npcLine: I woke in the rubble with no memory of him. Only this... phantom limb of the heart. A presence with no name. A love I could feel but not trace.

requiredGates.description: Completed pre-collapse arc

playerChoices.playerLine: Your body remembers even when your mind doesn't. That's not nothing.
playerChoices.elenyaResponse: My body remembers. And the remembering was killing me. I couldn't meditate through it. I couldn't breathe through it.

segments.title: The Choice to Release
segments.dialogueContext: Elenya describes her ritual of deliberate forgetting
segments.npcLine: I made a choice. Not out of cowardice. But out of mercy. I performed a ritual of forgetting—an act of release, a surrender of the half-memory that haunted me.

requiredGates.description: Player has processed Elenya's post-collapse loss with her
High narrative presence to understand the spiritual significance

playerChoices.playerLine: You didn't erase him. You released him to survive. That's not the same as betrayal.
playerChoices.elenyaResponse: I know. And yet... I still don't know if it was wisdom or the most profound betrayal. Perhaps it was both.

acts.description: Elenya becomes the High Seer, teaching compassion without attachment. But something in her trembles when asked about Malrik.

segments.title: Compassion Without Possession
segments.dialogueContext: Elenya teaches her followers. A student asks about attachment.
segments.npcLine: I teach compassion without attachment. Presence without possession. Love without expectation. But when followers ask about Malrik, my voice always falters.

requiredGates.description: Established post-collapse timeline

playerChoices.playerLine: Your trembling is the answer. You're teaching what you couldn't practice.
playerChoices.elenyaResponse: Yes. My students don't understand that the most profound teachings come from where we are broken. I teach what I failed to live.

segments.title: Body Knows
segments.dialogueContext: Elenya sees Malrik in the marketplace. Her body responds before her mind
segments.npcLine: When he walks through the marketplace, something in me recognizes. My chest tightens. My breath changes. My voice becomes careful, like holding something fragile.

requiredGates.description: Elenya has admitted her teaching contradicts her experience
Very high observation to notice Elenya's body memory

playerChoices.playerLine: Your body is wiser than your ritual of forgetting. It remembers what your mind can't.
playerChoices.elenyaResponse: My body betrays me. Or it saves me. I still don't know which.

segments.title: The Mountain Shrine: Where Presence Lives
segments.dialogueContext: Elenya speaks about her work at the shrine and what draws people to her
segments.npcLine: The shrine is not about ritual or doctrine. It's about bearing witness. People come here carrying weight they can't name. I listen. I hold space. And somehow, the weight becomes bearable.

requiredGates.description: Elenya's body has sensed Malrik's presence
Player can perceive Elenya's spiritual work

playerChoices.playerLine: You're describing what you do when Malrik is near—you bear witness. You hold space for his confusion.
playerChoices.elenyaResponse: I do. And it tears me apart, because I recognize exactly what he's confused about. It's the same ache I performed a ritual to forget.

playerChoices.playerLine: Your students don't know they're learning from someone who chose to forget the person she loved most. That's the deepest teaching.
playerChoices.elenyaResponse: They learn presence through my presence with them. And I teach release because I know the cost of holding on. Every word I speak about letting go comes from standing in his marketplace, calling him nothing but his name.

acts.description: A broken object becomes a key. The ritual of forgetting cracks open.

segments.title: Fragment and Echo
segments.dialogueContext: Elenya encounters a broken lighthouse lantern during ritual work
segments.npcLine: I found a broken lantern. Lighthouse, probably. I touched it and something caught in my throat. 'Someone I once knew would have fixed this. Just like that—illumination.' I laughed. And then I asked: What was his name?

requiredGates.description: Elenya's body has recognized presence

playerChoices.playerLine: The lantern—it's not asking you to remember his name. It's reminding you that you once knew it.
playerChoices.elenyaResponse: The ritual of forgetting is thinner than I thought. One object, one touch, and the walls crack.

segments.title: The Way of Presence and Release
segments.dialogueContext: Elenya speaks about her conflict with the Skeptics and what she knows about human healing
segments.npcLine: The Skeptics believe the world can be fixed through evidence and structure. But I've learned—through my own breaking—that some wounds need presence, not analysis. Meaning lives in the space between order and chaos.

requiredGates.description: Elenya's ritual of forgetting has cracked
Player has developed narrative understanding

playerChoices.playerLine: But he's teaching order because of the same wound. You're both trying to honor what you can't quite name.
playerChoices.elenyaResponse: Yes. His preservation is my release. His structure is my dissolution. And when I see him working in his Archive, cataloguing fragments... I see myself too. We're both grieving.

playerChoices.playerLine: Your philosophies aren't contradictions. They're two languages for the same truth about what it means to survive.
playerChoices.elenyaResponse: Then perhaps the building debate isn't about which approach is right. It's about learning to speak both languages. To honor both preservation and release.

acts.description: Elenya is drawn to the restored lighthouse. Malrik is there. The ache becomes a name.

segments.title: Recognition in Light
segments.dialogueContext: Elenya arrives at the restored lighthouse where Malrik stands
segments.npcLine: I didn't know why I came here. I only knew I had to. And when I saw him standing in the restored light, memory moved. Not clear. But felt. Undeniable.

requiredGates.description: Elenya's ritual of forgetting has begun to crack
Player has cultivated high empathy for this sacred moment

playerChoices.playerLine: You don't need the full memory back. He's here. That's enough.
playerChoices.elenyaResponse: But I owe him the truth. That I severed this deliberately. That I chose to forget him.

playerChoices.playerLine: You did what you had to do to survive. Can you forgive yourself for that?
playerChoices.elenyaResponse: That's the hardest forgiveness. But yes. I'm beginning to.

segments.title: The Choice Revealed
segments.dialogueContext: Elenya speaks to Malrik about her ritual of deliberate forgetting
segments.npcLine: I remember loving someone. And I remember choosing to forget because the half-memory was killing me. I didn't know it was you. But my heart did.

requiredGates.description: Elenya is ready to confess her deliberate forgetting
Elenya has accepted the necessity of her choice

playerChoices.playerLine: Your choice was an act of love. You saved yourself so you could be here now.
playerChoices.elenyaResponse: That's... yes. That's what I couldn't see before. The mercy of what I did. To let him go so he could become someone new. And so I could survive to meet him again.

segments.title: The Building as Covenant
segments.dialogueContext: Elenya speaks about the Shared Archive Building and what she wants it to represent
segments.npcLine: The building doesn't belong to the Skeptics or the Mystics. It belongs to both. I want to place offerings—flame, cloth, meaning—alongside his precise catalogues. Because both honor what was lost.

requiredGates.description: Elenya has accepted the necessity of her choice
Player has developed integrated perspective on both worldviews

playerChoices.playerLine: He fears that ritual will compromise the integrity of the Archive. But what if the ritual IS the integrity?
playerChoices.elenyaResponse: That's what I'm trying to show him. That sacred and preserved aren't opposites. They're different ways of honoring the same wound.

playerChoices.playerLine: You're not asking him to change his approach. You're asking him to expand it. To see that structure and meaning, preservation and presence, can coexist.
playerChoices.elenyaResponse: Yes. And I'm asking it by living it. By standing in the building with him. By being present while he preserves. By honoring what we lost together.

acts.description: Elenya holds the paradox of her choice. She did not betray. She survived. And somehow, they're here together.

segments.title: The Wisdom of What Was Lost
segments.dialogueContext: Elenya contemplates her ritual of forgetting and its necessity
segments.npcLine: I taught my students compassion without attachment. Presence without possession. But I was teaching from a wound, not wisdom. Now I understand: sometimes the most loving act is the one that lets go. And sometimes grace arrives as a second meeting.

requiredGates.description: Elenya has integrated the wisdom of her choice
Player has developed wisdom about paradox and mercy

playerChoices.playerLine: Your students can now learn from both—the teaching and the wound that taught it.
playerChoices.elenyaResponse: Yes. I can finally teach what I know instead of what I wish I'd lived. And that's its own kind of wholeness.

playerChoices.playerLine: You've become who you needed to become. Through the forgetting and back through the remembering.
playerChoices.elenyaResponse: We both have. He needed to grieve without knowing what he was grieving. I needed to survive by letting go. And now we're here—changed, wiser, ready for what comes next.

epilogueMoments.description: In the restored lighthouse, Elenya and Malrik stand together. She places her hand where his almost touches.
epilogueMoments.elenyaLine: I chose to forget you once. Not because you weren't worth remembering, but because remembering was burning me. Now I choose to remember—but differently. Not clinging to what was, but present for what is.
